Reports.headlineCv: Create a report for this CV.
Reports.headlineUser: Create a report for this user.
Reports.description: This report will be added to the moderation queue for review by a moderator or admin.
Reports.success: Report submitted. It now appears in the moderation queue.
Reports.validationError: The reason must contain at least 10 characters.
Reports.actionError: The report could not be created.

Landing.heroBadge: CV bank, HR review and moderation
Landing.heroTitle: Useful HR decisions, in one platform.
Landing.heroDescription: SAWA RH brings together CV intake, volunteer review, recruiter search and admin governance in one experience that stays easy to read and fast to use.
Landing.heroPanelTwoTitle: One control space for intake, review and sourcing.
Landing.heroPanelTwoDescription: Every critical action stays readable, signed and connected to the right role.
Landing.challengeTitle: HR teams lose time when their tools are scattered.
Landing.challengeDescription: Between CV uploads, reports, review requests and recruiter access, the product needs one clear surface that keeps everything together without overwhelming users.
Landing.challengeCardOneDescription: Uploads are categorized, stored privately and prepared for search without extra friction.
Landing.challengeCardTwoDescription: Volunteers can quickly see what to claim, process and submit.
Landing.challengeCardThreeDescription: Moderation, approvals and audit stay accessible without hidden screens.
Landing.toolsTitle: The modules you need to run the platform.
Landing.toolsDescription: Each block maps to a real SAWA RH use case: candidate intake, HR review and recruiter sourcing under control.
Landing.toolOneDescription: The candidate uploads a PDF or DOCX, adds a category and makes the profile usable right away.
Landing.toolTwoDescription: The volunteer HR pro opens the queue, claims a request and writes the full report on the same screen.
Landing.toolThreeDescription: Approved recruiters access a filtered, cleaner CV bank tied to signed documents.
Landing.planFootnote: HR_PRO and recruiter access still require admin verification before full activation.
Landing.integrationsDescription: The platform plugs into your real flow: API, storage, email and document processing.
Landing.faqOneQuestion: Who can access the CV bank?
Landing.faqOneAnswer: Verified HR_PRO users, approved recruiters, and admin or moderation roles according to their permissions.
Landing.faqTwoAnswer: CVs stay in a private bucket and are only served through signed URLs generated by the backend.
Landing.faqThreeQuestion: How does the HR review flow work?
Landing.faqThreeAnswer: The first eligible HR reviewer claims an open request, submits the report and the candidate is then notified.
Landing.newsletterDescription: Receive new openings, approvals and product updates so the platform stays easy to track.
Landing.newsletterCardTitle: New requests and approvals this week

Auth.registerDescription: Email + password registration, then guided onboarding.
Auth.loginRailTitle: Resume your CV pipeline, review requests and gated permissions without friction.
Auth.loginRailDescription: Sign-in opens a different workspace depending on role, completion level and verification status.
Auth.loginFooterNote: Email + password only. Strict RBAC. Automatic redirects based on profile state.
Auth.loginHighlightOneDescription: A candidate, an HR_PRO and a recruiter do not land on the same screens or receive the same access.
Auth.loginHighlightTwoDescription: Sensitive roles stay routed to pending state until the admin decision is complete.
Auth.loginHighlightThreeDescription: The CV bank stays partitioned and documents are delivered only through signed URLs.
Auth.registerRailTitle: Build a profile that is usable from the first session.
Auth.registerRailDescription: Registration is the entry point, then onboarding collects the data required for search, moderation and matching.
Auth.registerFooterNote: The selected role then drives verification rules and downstream screens.
Auth.registerHighlightOneDescription: Email, password, role and language are enough to unlock onboarding without unnecessary friction.
Auth.registerHighlightTwoDescription: The product sends the new account directly into profile completion after creation.
Auth.registerHighlightThreeDescription: HR_PRO and recruiter accounts then move into an explicit admin verification step.

Onboarding.title: Complete your profile
Onboarding.description: Fill in your information before using SAWA RH.
Onboarding.railTitle: A strong onboarding flow should prepare search, moderation and recruiter matching.
Onboarding.railDescription: This screen collects the data that powers verification, operational review and CV bank display.
Onboarding.footerNote: Once complete, the profile can be submitted for review depending on role.
Onboarding.highlightOneDescription: Name, location and contact details create the minimum usable profile for HR operations.
Onboarding.highlightTwoDescription: Headline, experience and category improve later search and fast resume scanning.
Onboarding.highlightThreeDescription: Roles under review move into the admin queue with visible status.
Onboarding.headline: Headline
Onboarding.pendingDescription: Your profile is currently under admin review.
Onboarding.pendingRailTitle: The profile stays gated until the admin decision lands.
Onboarding.pendingRailDescription: This checkpoint protects access to sensitive product areas including the HR queue and recruiter CV bank.

Dashboard.title: Dashboard
Dashboard.description: Open the right workspace quickly based on your role.
Dashboard.commandHeadline: One control center for every product state.
Dashboard.commandDescription: The dashboard is the switching point between candidate operations, the CV bank, the review queue and admin governance. The interface should make that network of decisions readable at a glance.
Dashboard.commandCardOneTitle: Upload, enrich and activate resumes without breaking the mobile flow.
Dashboard.commandCardTwoTitle: Move into full-text search with filters, sorting and signed access.
Dashboard.commandCardThreeTitle: Supervise the one-shot queue and route work to volunteer HR pros.
Dashboard.commandCardFourTitle: Keep validations and sensitive access under explicit oversight.
Dashboard.governanceDescription: Permissions, approvals and audit traces stay active across the platform.
Dashboard.governancePointOneDescription: Visible views change with the role, profile completion level and verification status.
Dashboard.governancePointTwoDescription: Searches, views and downloads must stay traceable to protect the CV bank.
Dashboard.governancePointThreeDescription: HR_PRO and recruiter approvals stay exposed as an operating surface, not a hidden screen.

AdminVerifications.title: Admin verifications
AdminVerifications.description: Process RH_PRO and RECRUITER profile validation requests.
AdminVerifications.opsHeadline: A validation panel built for fast decisions without losing context.
AdminVerifications.opsDescription: Admin review should expose the real profile state, completion level and prior decision trail while keeping approval and rejection one click away.
AdminVerifications.opsCardOneTitle: Approval opens HR_PRO or recruiter access. Rejection keeps the control checkpoint in place.
AdminVerifications.opsCardTwoTitle: Name, location, contact details and review note should stay visible before any decision.
AdminVerifications.opsCardThreeTitle: Every validation should remain tied to a reviewer, date and internal note.
AdminVerifications.opsCardFourTitle: Role and status filters keep the queue readable as the backlog grows.
AdminVerifications.filtersTitle: Shape the queue before processing profiles.
AdminVerifications.filtersDescription: Isolate a role type and verification state to keep the admin backlog easier to read and review.
AdminVerifications.queueNoteDescription: The selected role/status pair defines the set of profiles visible in this review session. Decisions then happen directly from each card.
AdminVerifications.decisionError: The decision could not be saved.
AdminVerifications.empty: No requests for the selected filters.
AdminVerifications.pendingActionDescription: Add internal context if needed, then approve or reject directly from this card.
AdminVerifications.notePlaceholder: Add context for the approval or rejection.

JobCategories.title: Job categories
JobCategories.description: Add job categories so CV upload becomes available right away.
JobCategories.formDescription: Admin and moderation can extend the job list without touching the database directly.
JobCategories.formHint: If the slug is empty, it will be generated automatically.
JobCategories.listDescription: Inactive categories no longer appear in CV creation.
JobCategories.createError: The category could not be created.
JobCategories.validationError: French and English names are required.

CVs.libraryDescription: Manage your private CVs, generate signed links and control visibility.
CVs.opsHeadline: A control surface for your resumes, not just a flat file list.
CVs.opsDescription: Each CV should read like an asset: visible status, file type, review eligibility and signed access to the original document.
CVs.opsCardOneTitle: Activate or hide a CV without losing its history or future usability.
CVs.opsCardTwoTitle: View or download the source file through backend-issued signed links.
CVs.opsCardThreeTitle: Only active CVs can enter the one-shot review queue.
CVs.opsCardFourTitle: Delete, hide and review actions stay consolidated on the same card.
CVs.resultsDescription: Each card groups document status, metadata and operational actions without forcing a detour through a separate screen.
CVs.actionError: The action on this CV failed.
CVs.uploadDescription: Upload a PDF or DOCX. Text will be extracted for future search.
CVs.uploadProfileComplete: Profile complete: the upload can proceed.
CVs.uploadProfileIncomplete: Profile still needs completion before advanced actions.
CVs.uploadOpsHeadline: The upload step should feel robust before the file is even sent.
CVs.uploadOpsDescription: This stage structures the CV, links a job category and prepares full-text indexing. The screen should reassure users about format, privacy and what happens next.
CVs.uploadOpsCardOneTitle: PDF and DOCX only, to preserve reliable text extraction.
CVs.uploadOpsCardTwoTitle: The selected category drives search quality and downstream HR reading.
CVs.uploadOpsCardThreeTitle: The document stays private and will only be served later through signed URLs.
CVs.uploadOpsCardFourTitle: Extracted text feeds the CV bank and the search layer.
CVs.formDescription: Title, category and file are enough to open a new CV cycle and make the document usable later.
CVs.uploadChecklistHeadline: What the system does after submission.
CVs.uploadChecklistDescription: The backend stores the document in a private bucket, extracts text and saves metadata for the CV bank.
CVs.uploadChecklistOneDescription: The flow rejects unsupported types and keeps the intake clean.
CVs.uploadChecklistTwoDescription: File content feeds search without exposing the storage path.
CVs.uploadChecklistThreeDescription: Pick a document to preview the last item ready to ship.
CVs.uploadChecklistThreeReady: This document is ready to be uploaded into your private library.
CVs.fileHint: Accepted formats: PDF or DOCX, max 10 MB.
CVs.requestBlocked: Activate the CV to request a review

Search.title: CV bank
Search.opsHeadline: A sourcing workspace closer to an analyst station than a simple form.
Search.opsDescription: Search should provide context, expose the current filtering level and make the state of the bank visible before users dive into results.
Search.opsCardOneTitle: Search against extracted text, profile signals and job categories.
Search.opsCardTwoTitle: View and download always go through signed, auditable URLs.
Search.opsCardThreeTitle: Switch between recent-first and relevance without leaving the screen.
Search.opsCardFourTitle: Only active, visible CVs feed the sourcing flow.
Search.filterDescription: Refine by keyword, category, location and experience.
Search.insightHeadline: Two indicators are enough to tell whether the query is too broad or too narrow.
Search.insightDescription: The screen should expose filter density and category coverage so sourcing can be adjusted before users even open candidate cards.
Search.insightPointOneTitle: Loose filtering increases noise, while tighter filtering improves quality but reduces coverage.
Search.insightPointTwoTitle: Every document access must stay compatible with confidentiality and audit constraints.
Search.loadError: The search could not be completed.

Moderation.title: Moderation and reports
Moderation.opsHeadline: An operator station to qualify, arbitrate and act quickly.
Moderation.opsDescription: This screen now reads live reports and exposes the critical actions directly: resolve, reject, hide a CV or ban a user.
Moderation.opsCardOneTitle: One flow handles both CV and user targets without losing context.
Moderation.opsCardTwoTitle: Each arbitration keeps a readable status and closure date.
Moderation.opsCardThreeTitle: Hiding a CV or banning an account remains explicit and readable.
Moderation.opsCardFourTitle: Moderation actions stay connected to the global audit trail.
Moderation.resolvedHint: This case has already been handled. Moderation actions are no longer available here.

filters.title: Filters
filters.headline: Filters
filters.description: Refine the queue by status and target.

Reviews.candidateDescription: Track your requests and read volunteer HR feedback.
Reviews.candidateOpsHeadline: A tracking space to understand every request state without opening multiple views.
Reviews.candidateOpsDescription: Candidates should understand what is in progress, what is assigned and what has already been delivered with scores and an actionable report.
Reviews.candidateOpsCardOneTitle: Each request retains its status, open date and assigned HR reviewer when applicable.
Reviews.candidateOpsCardTwoTitle: Once available, the three scores should be visible as a quick-read scoreboard.
Reviews.candidateOpsCardThreeTitle: The report returns summary, section notes, suggestions and a recommended template in the same card.
Reviews.candidateOpsCardFourTitle: When nothing is submitted yet, the screen should make that explicit.
Reviews.reviewFeedTitle: Read your requests as a clear pipeline.
Reviews.reviewFeedDescription: The cards below show request status, assigned HR and, when available, the full report without extra navigation.
Reviews.rhQueueDescription: Pick open requests, then submit your review.
Reviews.queueOpsHeadline: An operating panel to distribute, pick up and close reviews.
Reviews.queueOpsDescription: The HR queue should make workload readable, distinguish available demand from in-progress work and speed up takeover of priority requests.
Reviews.queueOpsCardOneTitle: The first eligible HR pro claims the request and removes it from the open pool.
Reviews.queueOpsCardTwoTitle: Cards should expose category, location and experience level without opening the detail.
Reviews.queueOpsCardThreeTitle: Keep the open backlog clearly separated from requests already in progress.
Reviews.queueOpsCardFourTitle: Submitting the review closes the loop and triggers the candidate notification.
Reviews.actionError: The action could not be completed.
Reviews.openQueueDescription: These requests have not been claimed yet. They can be acted on immediately by an eligible HR_PRO.
Reviews.myAssignedDescription: This area groups requests you already claimed and have not submitted yet.
Reviews.reportPendingDescription: The request exists, but no HR reviewer has submitted feedback yet. The status above shows where it currently sits in the queue.
Reviews.detailDescription: Review the CV, claim the request if it is still open, and produce a complete report without leaving this workspace.
Reviews.detailOpsHeadline: A working view built to read, assess and submit an actionable review.
Reviews.detailOpsDescription: The detail view should expose candidate context, extracted text and the report composer in one continuous flow. The goal is to reduce context switching during review.
Reviews.detailOpsCardOneTitle: The extracted CV text stays visible directly for fast document evaluation.
Reviews.detailOpsCardTwoTitle: An open request can be claimed here, then moves immediately into in-progress work.
Reviews.detailOpsCardThreeTitle: The three scores structure the feedback and create a quick read for the candidate.
Reviews.detailOpsCardFourTitle: The full report closes the loop and feeds the email notification.
Reviews.candidateBriefDescription: Read identity, contact details, location and experience before diving into the CV text.
Reviews.cvBriefDescription: This block keeps the document type, current status and request creation date visible.
Reviews.cvTextDescription: This text copy helps the reviewer scan the resume quickly without opening the original file.
Reviews.assignBlockDescription: Claim the request to remove it from the open backlog and reserve submission for your HR session.
Reviews.reportBlockHeadline: Raise a report without leaving the review.
Reviews.reportBlockDescription: If the CV or profile shows an issue, the report goes straight into the moderation queue.
Reviews.reviewComposerHeadline: Write the final report on the same screen.
Reviews.reviewComposerDescription: Fill scores, observations and recommendations. The report will attach to this request and trigger the candidate notification.
